text: The camera view remains close and centered on the working field, showing the da Vinci hand controls navigating the wire course. Frequent camera adjustments are made to minimize collisions, especially at two right-angle turns where a collision occurs but is corrected after passing the turn.

The surgeon navigates two curves using full wrist articulation on the da Vinci hand controls, resulting in visible collisions that suggest the camera position could be improved. The exercise requires precise finger-tip control and coordination for successful completion.
